summary: The pneumococcal vaccine PCV 23 polysaccharide demonstrates superior efficacy primarily in preventing diseases caused by S. pneumoniae infection, especially benefiting infants aged less than 2 years owing to their increased vulnerability and need for protective immunizations. This underscores why this group receives significant emphasis as target recipients within public health recommendations. \boxed{\text{children <2y}} Agent2 concludes based on these critical aspects related directly addressing infant immunities' needs versus other conditions mentioned which do not align closely enough with direct preventive outcomes demonstrated by PCV 23 usage patterns observed first when administering such vaccines aimed precisely towards combating most prevalent strains causing severe illness amongst pediatrics cohort worldwide thus ensuring maximum impact per dose administered thereby optimizing resource allocation efficiently while maximizing overall community immunity levels achieved collectively over time thanks to widespread implementation strategies employed consistently now widely accepted practice everywhere nowadays indeed proving highly effective tool combatting serious respiratory tract ailments threatening millions annually otherwise left
